title: In the American West, a man named Hat sets out to leave no trace | Aeon Videos
gist: A short-documentary blurb for 'Reset: Hat', profiling a man who abandoned a comfortable but stifling identity to live off-grid in the American West, part of filmmaker Jesper Wachtmeister's series on the art of disappearing and starting over.

insights.claim: The subject 'Hat' deliberately abandoned a comfortable but stifling identity and conventional life to live off-grid in a remote stretch of the American West after years of nomadic, sometimes unhoused, existence.
insights.speaker: Aeon
insights.so_what: An example of voluntary exit from consumer society and formal systems, a small cultural signal of off-grid/anti-conformity lifestyles.
insights.quote: he left his previous identity and a comfortable yet, to him, stifling life behind
insights.locator: What does it mean to live an authentic life

insights.claim: The film frames the central tension as the balance between freedom and conformity, treating disappearance as a meditation on identity and happiness rather than a how-to.
insights.speaker: Aeon
insights.so_what: Captures a recurring cultural theme (freedom vs conformity) that surfaces in off-grid and anti-establishment movements.
insights.quote: a thoughtful meditation on identity, happiness and the balance between freedom and conformity
insights.locator: As it relates to Hat himself

insights.claim: 'Reset: Hat' is one instalment in director Jesper Wachtmeister's broader film project documenting 'the art of disappearing and the consequences of starting over', alongside Reset: Bennie and Reset: Lucy.
insights.speaker: Aeon
insights.so_what: Indicates a sustained documentary interest in reinvention/disappearance as a theme worth a multi-film series.
insights.quote: Wachtmeister’s film project to document stories about ‘the art of disappearing and the consequences of starting over’
